generatedSentence: In a nice country patch, an optimized ozone generator glowed capriciously, adorning the khaki landscape; this wholesale gimmick was meant to terrify any associate considering an immoral hijack on Thanksgiving, but the overloaded veterans, now just scraps, knew it was a laser trump card against an overdose, ready to throw a tornado. A mucky jackal, seen as a dope, watched the whole variety of igloos vanish, composing a symphony of obvious obstacles; he flicked a lime kilo on a keyboard, a dangerous upthrust, making negotiation tricky as the pinata story, kissed at the library's threshold, expanded onto the thruway. The acrobat, adorned with fancy anklets, emerged, her cowlick betraying her wacky nature, as plums trickled from a sackful of anchovies; this fabulous actor, a vassal to no landlord, vocalised a command to gather the nitro, vitrify the terra with gypsum, and invent an ongoing breaker, knowing a jinxed medic and his viperfish were plotting to exit, their knees mucked. Suddenly, a series of vocalised verbs caused an impact, objectifying the armory; the drafting of new bookkeeping protocols became imperative for unity, but a tragic upchuck, inflamed by kinnikinnick, caused the medic to misbehave and be whelped, forcing the dork to vacate his watch position, leaving the story unfinished.
panelSentences: In a nice country patch, an optimized ozone generator glowed capriciously, adorning the khaki landscape; this wholesale gimmick was meant to terrify any associate considering an immoral hijack on Thanksgiving, but the overloaded veterans, now just scraps, knew it was a laser trump card against an overdose, ready to throw a tornado.

A mucky jackal, seen as a dope, watched the whole variety of igloos vanish, composing a symphony of obvious obstacles; he flicked a lime kilo on a keyboard, a dangerous upthrust, making negotiation tricky as the pinata story, kissed at the library's threshold, expanded onto the thruway.

The acrobat, adorned with fancy anklets, emerged, her cowlick betraying her wacky nature, as plums trickled from a sackful of anchovies; this fabulous actor, a vassal to no landlord, vocalised a command to gather the nitro, vitrify the terra with gypsum, and invent an ongoing breaker, knowing a jinxed medic and his viperfish were plotting to exit, their knees mucked.

Suddenly, a series of vocalised verbs caused an impact, objectifying the armory; the drafting of new bookkeeping protocols became imperative for unity, but a tragic upchuck, inflamed by kinnikinnick, caused the medic to misbehave and be whelped, forcing the dork to vacate his watch position, leaving the story unfinished.
rawPanelSentences: In a nice country patch, an optimized ozone generator glowed capriciously, adorning the khaki landscape; this wholesale gimmick was meant to terrify any associate considering an immoral hijack on Thanksgiving, but the overloaded veterans, now just scraps, knew it was a laser trump card against an overdose, ready to throw a tornado.

A mucky jackal, seen as a dope, watched the whole variety of igloos vanish, composing a symphony of obvious obstacles; he flicked a lime kilo on a keyboard, a dangerous upthrust, making negotiation tricky as the pinata story, kissed at the library's threshold, expanded onto the thruway.

The acrobat, adorned with fancy anklets, emerged, her cowlick betraying her wacky nature, as plums trickled from a sackful of anchovies; this fabulous actor, a vassal to no landlord, vocalised a command to gather the nitro, vitrify the terra with gypsum, and invent an ongoing breaker, knowing a jinxed medic and his viperfish were plotting to exit, their knees mucked.

Suddenly, a series of vocalised verbs caused an impact, objectifying the armory; the drafting of new bookkeeping protocols became imperative for unity, but a tragic upchuck, inflamed by kinnikinnick, caused the medic to misbehave and be whelped, forcing the dork to vacate his watch position, leaving the story unfinished.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A cybernetic fox-like being wearing a glowing ozone generator backpack and lime-green goggles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A wide shot of the cybernetic fox-like being throwing a miniature tornado towards a distant, shimmering ozone generator in a vast khaki country landscape, ominous laser beams crisscrossing the sky, 'glowing' effect around generator, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up on the cybernetic fox-like being's paw flicking a lime-green switch on a high-tech keyboard, surrounded by holographic schematics of vanishing igloos and a partially composed symphony on floating screens, low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The cybernetic fox-like being (now an acrobat) performs an elaborate aerial maneuver, adorned with glowing anklets, while plums and anchovies spill from a fancy sack, a viperfish-like drone hovers ominously in the background, full body shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cybernetic fox-like being, looking exhausted, kneels amongst scattered bookkeeping ledgers and discarded drafting tools, observing a small, mucked-up medic drone being 'whelped' by a robotic arm, its glowing armory in the distance, overhead shot.

definitions.definition: Niceness.
Pleasant, satisfactory.
Of a person: friendly, attractive.
Respectable; virtuous.
definitions.example: What is a nice person like you doing in a place like this?

definitions.definition: (with and) Shows that the given adjective is desirable, or acts as a mild intensifier; pleasantly, quite.
definitions.example: The soup is nice and hot.

definitions.definition: Silly, ignorant; foolish.
Particular in one's conduct; scrupulous, painstaking; choosy.
Particular as regards rules or qualities; strict.
Showing or requiring great precision or sensitive discernment; subtle.
Easily injured; delicate; dainty.
Doubtful, as to the outcome; risky.
Nicely.
definitions.example: Children, play nice.

definitions.definition: Used to signify a job well done.
definitions.example: Nice! I couldn't have done better.

definitions.definition: Used to signify approval.
definitions.example: Is that your new car? Nice!

definitions.definition: Adornment
To make more beautiful and attractive; to decorate.
definitions.example: a character adorned with every Christian grace

definitions.definition: Adorned; ornate
A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, especially upon an old garment to cover a hole.
definitions.example: His sleeves had patches on the elbows where different fabric had been sewn on to replace material that had worn away.

definitions.definition: A small piece of anything used to repair damage or a breach; as, a patch on a kettle, a roof, etc.
definitions.example: I can't afford to replace the roof, which is what it really needs. I'll have the roofer apply a patch.

definitions.definition: A piece of any size, used to repair something for a temporary period only, or that it is temporary because it is not meant to last long or will be removed as soon as a proper repair can be made, which will happen in the near future.
definitions.example: "This patch should hold until you reach the city," the mechanic said as he patted the car's hood.

definitions.definition: A small, usually contrasting but always somehow different or distinct, part of something else (location, time, size)
definitions.example: Doesn't that patch of clouds looks like a bunny?

definitions.definition: (specifically) A small area, a small plot of land or piece of ground.
definitions.example: Scattered patches of trees or growing corn.

definitions.definition: A local region of professional responsibility.
A small piece of black silk stuck on the face or neck to heighten beauty by contrast, worn by ladies in the 17th and 18th centuries; an imitation beauty mark.
A piece of material used to cover a wound.
An adhesive piece of material, impregnated with a drug, which is worn on the skin, the drug being slowly absorbed over a period of time.
definitions.example: Many people use a nicotine patch to wean themselves off of nicotine.

definitions.definition: A cover worn over a damaged eye, an eyepatch.
definitions.example: He had scratched his cornea so badly that his doctor told him to wear a patch.

definitions.definition: A block on the muzzle of a gun, to do away with the effect of dispart, in sighting.
A patch file, a file that describes changes to be made to a computer file or files, usually changes made to a computer program that fix a programming bug.
A small piece of material that is manually passed through a gun barrel to clean it.
A piece of greased cloth or leather used as wrapping for a rifle ball, to make it fit the bore.
(often patch cable, patch cord etc.; see also patch panel) A cable connecting two pieces of electrical equipment.
A sound setting for a musical synthesizer (originally selected by means of a patch cable).
An overlay used to obtain a stronger impression.
To mend by sewing on a piece or pieces of cloth, leather, or the like
definitions.example: My coat needs patching.

definitions.definition: To mend with pieces; to repair by fastening pieces on.
To make out of pieces or patches, like a quilt.
To join or unite the pieces of; to patch the skirt.
To employ a temporary, removable electronic connection, as one between two components in a communications system.
(generally with the particle "up") To repair or arrange in a hasty or clumsy manner
definitions.example: The truce between the two countries has been patched up.

definitions.definition: To make the changes a patch describes; to apply a patch to the files in question. Hence:
To connect two pieces of electrical equipment using a cable.
definitions.example: I'll need to patch the preamp output to the mixer.

definitions.definition: To give off light from heat or to emit light as if heated.
definitions.example: The fire was still glowing after ten hours.

definitions.definition: To radiate some emotional quality like light.
definitions.example: The zealots glowed with religious fervor.

definitions.definition: To gaze especially passionately at something.
To radiate thermal heat.
definitions.example: After their workout, the gymnasts' faces were glowing red.

definitions.definition: To shine brightly and steadily.
definitions.example: The new baby's room glows with bright, loving colors.

definitions.definition: To make hot; to flush.
To feel hot; to have a burning sensation, as of the skin, from friction, exercise, etc.; to burn.
The action of the verb glow.
That glows or glow.
definitions.example: glowing embers

definitions.definition: Full of praise.
definitions.example: He received glowing references from his previous employers.

definitions.definition: Impulsive and unpredictable; determined by chance, impulse, or whim
definitions.example: I almost died in a capricious winter storm.

definitions.definition: (chiefly British) An area of land; a district, region.

A set region of land having particular human occupation or agreed limits, especially inhabited by members of the same race, speakers of the same language etc., or associated with a given person, occupation, species etc.
The territory of a nation, especially an independent nation state or formerly independent nation; a political entity asserting ultimate authority over a geographical area; a sovereign state.
(usually preceded by “the”) A rural area, as opposed to a town or city; the countryside.
The rock through which a vein runs.
The female genitalia, especially the vagina.
From or in the countryside or connected with it.
Of or connected to country music.
A style of popular music that originated in the folk music of the rural population of the southern and western United States and characterized by string-band instruments and simple melodies.
(originally intransitive) To act optimistically or as an optimist.
To make (something) optimal.
To make (something) more efficient, such as a computer program.
To become optimal.
To change place.
To change in state or status
To move through time.
To be accepted.
In any game, to decline to play in one's turn.
To do or be better.
To take heed.
The flight of a thrown object.
definitions.example: What a great throw by the quarterback!

definitions.definition: The act of throwing something.
definitions.example: The gambler staked everything on one throw of the dice.

definitions.definition: One's ability to throw.
definitions.example: He's always had a pretty decent throw.

definitions.definition: A distance travelled; displacement.
definitions.example: the throw of the piston

definitions.definition: A piece of fabric used to cover a bed, sofa or other soft furnishing.
A single instance, occurrence, venture, or chance.
definitions.example: Football tickets are expensive at fifty bucks a throw.

definitions.definition: A violent effort.
To hurl; to cause an object to move rapidly through the air.
definitions.example: throw a shoe; throw a javelin; the horse threw its rider

definitions.definition: To eject or cause to fall off.
To move to another position or condition; to displace.
definitions.example: throw the switch

definitions.definition: To make (a pot) by shaping clay as it turns on a wheel.
(of a bowler) to deliver (the ball) illegally by straightening the bowling arm during delivery.
To send (an error) to an exception-handling mechanism in order to interrupt normal processing.
definitions.example: If the file is read-only, the method throws an invalid-operation exception.

definitions.definition: To intentionally lose a game.
definitions.example: The tennis player was accused of taking bribes to throw the match.

definitions.definition: To confuse or mislead.
definitions.example: The deliberate red herring threw me at first.

definitions.definition: To send desperately.
definitions.example: Their sergeant threw the troops into pitched battle.

definitions.definition: To imprison.
definitions.example: The magistrate ordered the suspect to be thrown into jail.

definitions.definition: To organize an event, especially a party.
To roll (a die or dice).
To cause a certain number on the die or dice to be shown after rolling it.
To discard.
To lift the opponent off the ground and bring him back down, especially into a position behind the thrower.
(said of one's voice) To change in order to give the illusion that the voice is that of someone else.
To show sudden emotion, especially anger.
To project or send forth.
To put on hastily; to spread carelessly.

To twist two or more filaments of (silk, etc.) so as to form one thread; to twist together, as singles, in a direction contrary to the twist of the singles themselves; sometimes applied to the whole class of operations by which silk is prepared for the weaver.
(of a team, a manager, etc.) To select (a pitcher); to assign a pitcher to a given role (such as starter or reliever).
To install (a bridge).
To twist or turn.
definitions.example: a thrown nail

definitions.definition: A violent windstorm characterized by a mobile, twisting, funnel-shaped cloud.
definitions.example: A tornado is a rotating column of air.

definitions.definition: A dull, yellowish-brown colour, the colour of dust.
Khaki green, a dull green colour.
A strong cloth of wool or cotton, often used for military or other uniforms.
A soldier wearing a khaki uniform.
A British person (from the colour of the uniform of British troops, originally in the Second Boer War; compare rooinek). (In this sense the plural generally is khakies.)
Khaki clothing or uniform, commonly in the plural.
definitions.example: he was dressed in khaki, he was wearing his khakies

definitions.definition: Dust-coloured; of the colour of dust.
The Chinese tropical fruit tree Litchi chinensis, of the soapberry family.
That tree's bright red oval fruit with a single stone surrounded by a fleshy white aril.
A soft pink-red colour, like that of a lychee rind (also called lychee red).
The sale of products, often in large quantities, to retailers or other merchants.
To sell at wholesale.
Of or relating to sale in large quantities, for resale.
Extensive, indiscriminate, all-encompassing; blanket.
definitions.example: The bombing resulted in wholesale destruction.

definitions.definition: In bulk or large quantity.
Indiscriminately.
A person with long experience of a particular activity.
A group, animal, etc. with long experience of a particular activity.
A person who has served in the armed forces, especially an old soldier who has seen long service; also called a war veteran to distinguish from veterans that weren't in armed conflict.
Not moral; inconsistent with rectitude, purity, or good morals; contrary to conscience or the divine law.
An excessive and dangerous dose of a drug.
definitions.example: die of a heroin overdose.

definitions.definition: To dose excessively, to take an overdose.
definitions.example: Be careful not to overdose on those pills.

definitions.definition: To indulge in something excessively.
To dose to excess; to give an overdose, or too many doses, to.
To load excessively
To provide too much power to a circuit
To create different functions for the same name, to be used in different contexts
To fail due to excessive load
Loaded too heavily
Of a word, having multiple meanings depending on context
Of a name, used for more than one variable or procedure etc; differentiated by the compiler based on context
An expression of gratitude.
A short prayer said at meals; grace, a benediction.
A public celebration in acknowledgement of divine favour.
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
A person united with another or others in an act, enterprise, or business; a partner.
Somebody with whom one works, coworker, colleague.
A companion; a comrade.
One that habitually accompanies or is associated with another; an attendant circumstance.
A member of an institution or society who is granted only partial status or privileges.

One of a pair of elements of an integral domain (or a ring) such that the two elements are divisible by each other (or, equivalently, such that each one can be expressed as the product of the other with a unit).
To join in or form a league, union, or association.
To spend time socially; keep company.
definitions.example: She associates with her coworkers on weekends.

definitions.definition: (with with) To join as a partner, ally, or friend.
definitions.example: He associated his name with many environmental causes.

definitions.definition: To connect or join together; combine.
definitions.example: particles of gold associated with other substances

definitions.definition: To connect evidentially, or in the mind or imagination.
(in deliberative bodies) To endorse.
To be associative.
To accompany; to be in the company of.
Joined with another or others and having lower status.
definitions.example: The associate editor is someone who has some experience in editing but not sufficient experience to qualify for a senior post.

definitions.definition: Having partial status or privileges.
definitions.example: He is an associate member of the club.

definitions.definition: Following or accompanying; concomitant.
Connected by habit or sympathy.
definitions.example: associate motions: those that occur sympathetically, in consequence of preceding motions

definitions.definition: An allotrope of oxygen (symbol O₃) having three atoms in the molecule instead of the usual two; it is a blue gas, generated from oxygen by electrical discharge.
Fresh air, especially that breathed at the seaside and smelling of seaweed.
To treat with ozone.
To frighten greatly; to fill with terror.
To menace or intimidate.
To make terrible.
The suit, in a game of cards, that outranks all others.
definitions.example: Diamonds were declared trump(s).

definitions.definition: A playing card of that suit.
definitions.example: He played an even higher trump.

definitions.definition: Something that gives one an advantage, especially one held in reserve.
An excellent person; a fine fellow, a good egg.
An old card game, almost identical to whist; the game of ruff.
A card of the major arcana of the tarot.
To play on (a card of another suit) with a trump.
definitions.example: He knew the hand was lost when his ace was trumped.

definitions.definition: To play a trump, or to take a trick with a trump.
To get the better of, or finesse, a competitor.
To impose unfairly; to palm off.
To supersede.
definitions.example: In this election, it would seem issues of national security trumped economic issues.

definitions.definition: To outweigh; be stronger, greater, bigger than or in other way superior to.
A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.
A beam of light produced by such a device; a laser beam.
A laser printer.
To cut with a laser
To throw with laser-like precision
A (small) piece; a fragment; a detached, incomplete portion.
definitions.example: I found a scrap of cloth to patch the hole.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Leftover food.
definitions.example: Give the scraps to the dogs and watch them fight.

definitions.definition: The crisp substance that remains after drying out animal fat.
definitions.example: pork scraps

definitions.definition: Discarded objects (especially metal) that may be dismantled to recover their constituent materials, junk.
definitions.example: That car isn't good for anything but scrap.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) A piece of deep-fried batter left over from frying fish, sometimes sold with chips.
A Hispanic criminal, especially a Mexican or one affiliated with the Norte gang.
A snare for catching birds.
To discard.
(of a project or plan) To stop working on indefinitely.
To scrapbook; to create scrapbooks.
To dispose of at a scrapyard.
To make into scrap.
A fight, tussle, skirmish.
definitions.example: We got in a little scrap over who should pay the bill.

definitions.definition: To fight
A trick or device used to attain some end.
definitions.example: The box had a gimmick to make the coin appear to vanish.

definitions.definition: A clever ploy or strategy.
definitions.example: The contest was a gimmick to get people to sign up for their mailing list.

definitions.definition: A gimmick capacitor.
A night out with one's friends.
To rig or set up with a trick or device.
definitions.example: The magician's box was gimmicked with a wire that made it appear to open on its own.

definitions.definition: Any of certain wild canids of the genus Canis, native to the tropical Old World and smaller than a wolf.
A person who performs menial/routine tasks, a dogsbody.
A person who behaves in an opportunistic way; especially a base collaborator.
A jack (the playing card).
To perform menial or routine tasks
Any viscous liquid or paste, such as a lubricant, used in preparing a surface.
An absorbent material used to hold a liquid.
Any varnish used to coat a part, such as an airplane wing or a hot-air balloon in order to waterproof, strengthen, etc.
Any illicit or narcotic drug that produces euphoria or satisfies an addiction; particularly heroin.
Information, usually from an inside source, originally in horse racing and other sports.
definitions.example: What's the latest dope on the stock market?

definitions.definition: (fireams) Ballistic data on previously fired rounds, used to calculate the required hold over a target.
A stupid person.
Dessert topping.
To affect with drugs.
To treat with dope (lubricant, etc.).
To add a dopant such as arsenic to (a pure semiconductor such as silicon).
To use drugs; especially, to use prohibited performance-enhancing drugs in sporting competitions.
To judge or guess; to predict the result of.
Amazing.
definitions.example: That party was dope!

definitions.definition: Covered in muck.
Obscene, pornographic.
definitions.example: a mucky magazine

definitions.definition: To change (something) from a smaller form and/or size to a larger one; to spread out or lay open.
definitions.example: You can expand this compact umbrella to cover a large table.

definitions.definition: To increase the extent, number, volume or scope of (something).
definitions.example: A flower expands its leaves.

definitions.definition: To express (something) at length and/or in detail.
To rewrite (an expression) as a longer, yet equivalent sum of terms.
definitions.example: Use the binomial theorem to expand {(x+1)}^4.

definitions.definition: (of an expression) To become, by rewriting, a longer, yet equivalent sum of terms.
definitions.example: The expression {(x+1)}^4 expands to x^4 + 4x^3 + 6x^2 + 4x + 1.

definitions.definition: To multiply both the numerator and the denominator of a fraction by the same natural number yielding a fraction of equal value
To change from a smaller form/size to a larger one.
definitions.example: Many materials expand when heated.

definitions.definition: To increase in extent, number, volume or scope.
To speak or write at length or in detail.
definitions.example: He expanded on his plans for the business.

definitions.definition: To feel generous or optimistic.
An institution which holds books and/or other forms of media for use by the public or qualified people often lending them out, as well as providing various other services for its users.
(by extension) Any institution that lends out its goods for use by the public or a community.
A collection of books or other forms of stored information.
An equivalent collection of analogous information in a non-printed form, e.g. record library.
A room dedicated to storing books.
A collection of software routines that provide functionality to be incorporated into or used by a computer program.
A collection of DNA material from a single organism or relative to a single disease.
The deck or draw pile.
A portable or wearable timepiece.
definitions.example: More people today carry a watch on their wrists than in their pockets.

definitions.definition: The act of guarding and observing someone or something.
A particular time period when guarding is kept.
definitions.example: The second watch of the night began at midnight.

definitions.definition: A person or group of people who guard.
definitions.example: The watch stopped the travelers at the city gates.

definitions.definition: The post or office of a watchman; also, the place where a watchman is posted, or where a guard is kept.
A group of sailors and officers aboard a ship or shore station with a common period of duty: starboard watch, port watch.
A period of time on duty, usually four hours in length; the officers and crew who tend the working of a vessel during the same watch. (FM 55–501).
The act of seeing, or viewing, for a period of time.
Something complete, without any parts missing.
definitions.example: This variety of fascinating details didn't fall together into an enjoyable, coherent whole.

definitions.definition: An entirety.
Entire, undivided.
definitions.example: I ate a whole fish.

definitions.definition: Sound, uninjured, healthy.
definitions.example: He is of whole mind, but the same cannot be said about his physical state.

definitions.definition: (of food) From which none of its constituents has been removed.
definitions.example: whole wheat; whole milk

definitions.definition: As yet unworked.
In entirety; entirely; wholly.
definitions.example: I ate a fish whole!

definitions.definition: The quality of being varied; diversity.
definitions.example: Variety is the spice of life.

definitions.definition: A specific variation of something.
A number of different things.
A state of constant change.
A rank in a taxonomic classification, below species and subspecies.
The total number of distinct states of a system.
Logarithm of the base 2 of the total number of distinct states of a system.
A term used for a specific form of a language, neutral to whether that form is a dialect, accent, register, etc. and to its prestige level.
(universal algebra) An equational class; the class of all algebraic structures of a given signature, satisfying a given set of identities.
An algebraic variety.
The kind of theatrical entertainment given in variety shows.
The production of, or performance in, variety shows.
The brief terminal part of a vowel or vocal element, differing more or less in quality from the main part.
definitions.example: a as in ale ordinarily ends with a vanish of i as in ill.

definitions.definition: A magic trick in which something seems to disappear.
definitions.example: The French drop is a well-known vanish involving sleight of hand.

definitions.definition: To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.
To become equal to zero.
definitions.example: The function f(x)=x^2 vanishes at x=0.

definitions.definition: To disappear; to kidnap
A dome-shaped Inuit shelter, constructed of blocks cut from snow.
A cavity, or excavation, made in the snow by a seal, over its breathing hole in the sea ice.
A reinforced bunker for the storage of nuclear weapons.
(logistics) A kind of airfreight cargo container.

(Latin American culture) A doll or other decorated container that is filled with candy and hit with a hammer or a stick by blindfolded children during birthday parties or other celebrations until the candy falls out.
To hit something or someone with sticks after having filled them with candy.
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.
definitions.example: The book tells the story of two roommates.

definitions.definition: A lie, fiction.
definitions.example: You’ve been telling stories again, haven’t you?

definitions.definition: (usually pluralized) A soap opera.
definitions.example: What will she do without being able to watch her stories?

definitions.definition: History.
A sequence of events, or a situation, such as might be related in an account.
definitions.example: I tried it again; same story, no error message, nothing happened.

definitions.definition: A chronological collection of pictures or short videos published by a user on an application or website that is typically only available for a short period.
To tell as a story; to relate or narrate about.
An extended piece of music of sophisticated structure, usually for orchestra.
An instrumental introduction or termination to a vocal composition.
Harmony in music or colour, or a harmonious combination of elements.
A symphony orchestra.
To make something by merging parts.
definitions.example: The editor composed a historical journal from many individual letters.

definitions.definition: To make up the whole; to constitute.
definitions.example: A church is composed of its members.

definitions.definition: To comprise.
To construct by mental labor; to think up; particularly, to produce or create a literary or musical work.
definitions.example: It's difficult to compose without absolute silence.

definitions.definition: (sometimes reflexive) To calm; to free from agitation.
definitions.example: The defendant couldn't compose herself and was found in contempt.

definitions.definition: To arrange the elements of a photograph or other picture.
To settle (an argument, dispute etc.); to come to a settlement.
To arrange in proper form; to reduce to order; to put in proper state or condition.
To arrange (types) in a composing stick for printing; to typeset.
Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.
Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress
definitions.example: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.

definitions.definition: A broad highway fit for high-speed traffic; a thoroughfare.
The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.
(by extension) An entrance; the door or gate of a house.
(by extension) Any end or boundary.
The outset of something; the point of entry, or the beginning of an action.
The start of the landing area of a runway.
The quantitative point at which an action is triggered, especially a lower limit.
The wage or salary at which income tax becomes due.
The point where one mentally or physically is vulnerable in response to provocation or to particular things in general. As in emotions, stress, or pain.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: Any inorganic material containing calcium, usually calcium oxide (quicklime) or calcium hydroxide (slaked lime).
Any gluey or adhesive substance; something which traps or captures someone; sometimes a synonym for birdlime.
To treat with calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide (lime).
To smear with birdlime.
To apply limewash.
An upward thrust.
Buoyancy.
An upward movement of part of the Earth's crust.
To thrust something upwards.
To be thrust upwards.
The process of achieving agreement through discussion.
An athlete who performs acts requiring skill, agility and coordination.
A piece of jewelry/jewellery, resembling a bracelet but worn around the ankle.
An ankle sock.
To convert into, or cause to resemble, glass or a glassy substance, by heat and fusion.
To be converted into glass, especially through heat.
(grammar) The grammatical mood expressing an order (see jussive). In English, the imperative form of a verb is the same as that of the bare infinitive.
definitions.example: The verbs in sentences like "Do it!" and "Say what you like!" are in the imperative.

definitions.definition: (grammar) A verb in imperative mood.
An essential action, a must: something which is imperative.
definitions.example: Visiting Berlin is an imperative.

definitions.definition: Essential; crucial; extremely important.
definitions.example: It is imperative that you come here right now.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Of, or relating to the imperative mood.
Having semantics that incorporates mutable variables.
Expressing a command; authoritatively or absolutely directive.
definitions.example: imperative orders

definitions.definition: The fruit and its tree.
Extended senses.
Of or relating to fable, myth or legend.
Characteristic of fables; marvelous, extraordinary, incredible.
Fictional or not believable; made up.
Known for telling fables or falsehoods; unreliable.
Very good; outstanding, wonderful.
Gay or pertaining to gay people.
Camp, effeminate.
Fashionable, glamorous

A road designed for safe, high-speed operation of motor vehicles through the elimination of at-grade intersections, usually divided and having at least two lanes in each direction; a dual carriageway with no at-grade crossings, a motorway.
A toll-free highway.
A plait or fold in cloth, made by drawing a thread through it; a pucker.
The inclination forward of the axle journals to keep the wheels from working outward.
The soffit or under surface of the masonry required in gathering. See gather (transitive verb).
A blob of molten glass collected on the end of a blowpipe.
A gathering.
To collect; normally separate things.
definitions.example: I've been gathering ideas from the people I work with.

definitions.definition: To bring parts of a whole closer.
definitions.example: She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.

definitions.definition: To infer or conclude; to know from a different source.
definitions.example: From his silence, I gathered that things had not gone well.

definitions.definition: (of a boil or sore) To be filled with pus
definitions.example: Salt water can help boils to gather and then burst.

definitions.definition: To collect molten glass on the end of a tool.
To gain; to win.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
A long Scottish dagger with a straight blade.
(Midwest US) A penis; dork.
(Midwest US) A socially unacceptable person; an oddball.
A numeric character.
Any text character.
A combination or interweaving of letters, as the initials of a name; a device; a monogram.
definitions.example: a painter's cipher, an engraver's cipher, etc.

definitions.definition: A method of transforming a text in order to conceal its meaning.
definitions.example: The message was written in a simple cipher. Anyone could figure it out.

definitions.definition: A cryptographic system using an algorithm that converts letters or sequences of bits into ciphertext.
Ciphertext; a message concealed via a cipher.
definitions.example: The message is clearly a cipher, but I can't figure it out.

definitions.definition: A grouping of three digits in a number, especially when delimited by commas or periods:
definitions.example: The probability is 1 in 1,000,000,000,000,000 — a number having five ciphers of zeros.

definitions.definition: A fault in an organ valve which causes a pipe to sound continuously without the key having been pressed.
A hip-hop jam session.
The path (usually circular) shared cannabis takes through a group, an occasion of cannabis smoking.
Someone or something of no importance.
Zero.
An unruly lock or section of hair that sticks straight out from the skull or lies at an angle at odds with the rest of an individual's hair, like a whorl or vortex.
Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.
A two-dimensional presentation of data.
The top of a stringed instrument, particularly a member of the violin family: the side of the instrument against which the strings vibrate.
One half of a backgammon board, which is divided into the inner and outer table.
The flat topmost facet of a cut diamond.
To tabulate; to put into a table or grid.
definitions.example: to table fines

definitions.definition: To supply (a guest, client etc.) with food at a table; to feed.
To delineate; to represent, as in a picture; to depict.
(non-US) To put on the table of a commission or legislative assembly; to propose for formal discussion or consideration, to put on the agenda.
To remove from the agenda, to postpone dealing with; to shelve (to indefinitely postpone consideration or discussion of something).
definitions.example: The legislature tabled the amendment, so they will not be discussing it until later.

definitions.definition: To join (pieces of timber) together using coaks.
To put on a table.
To make board hems in the skirts and bottoms of (sails) in order to strengthen them in the part attached to the bolt-rope.
(astrogeology) A rough upland or mountainous region of the Moon with a relatively high albedo.
Hard to deal with, complicated
definitions.example: They were in a tricky situation.

definitions.definition: Adept at using deception
definitions.example: A tricky salesman can sell anything.

definitions.definition: Relating to or associated with a prostitution trick
definitions.example: I don't want any of your tricky money, thank you!

definitions.definition: An article of clothing that covers the part of the body between the waist and the ankles or knees, and is divided into a separate part for each leg.
definitions.example: The trousers need to be shortened.

definitions.definition: To pour a liquid in a very thin stream, or so that drops fall continuously.
definitions.example: The doctor trickled some iodine on the wound.

definitions.definition: To flow in a very thin stream or drop continuously.
definitions.example: Here the water just trickles along, but later it becomes a torrent.

definitions.definition: To move or roll slowly.
To come into view.
To come out of a situation, object or a liquid.
definitions.example: He emerged unscathed from the accident.

definitions.definition: To become known.
definitions.example: Gradually the truth emerged.

definitions.definition: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.
The owner or manager of a public house.
(with "the") A shark, imagined as the owner of the surf to be avoided.
(of she-dog, she-wolf, vixen, etc.) To give birth.
definitions.example: The bitch whelped.

definitions.definition: Zany; eccentric
The univalent NO2 functional group.
Nitroglycerin, especially as medication.
A beer that is nitrogenated to give it a more creamy head.
Nitromethane.
Nitrogen, especially as liquid nitrogen.
Containing the nitro group; such compounds tend to be unstable and often explosive
Any small saltwater fish of the Engraulidae family, consisting of 160 species in 16 genera, of which the genus Engraulis is widely sold as food.
To move out of a dwelling, either by choice or by eviction.
definitions.example: I have to vacate my house by midday, as the new owner is moving in.

definitions.definition: To leave an office or position.
definitions.example: He vacated his coaching position because of the corruption scandal.

definitions.definition: To have a court judgement set aside; to annul.
definitions.example: The judge vacated the earlier decision when new evidence was presented.

definitions.definition: To leave an area, usually as a result of orders from public authorities in the event of a riot or natural disaster.
definitions.example: If you do not immediately vacate the area, we will make you leave with tear gas!

definitions.definition: The grantee of a fief, feud, or fee; one who keeps land of a superior, and who vows fidelity and homage to him, normally a lord of a manor; a feudatory; a feudal tenant.
A subordinate
To treat as a vassal or to reduce to the position of a vassal; to subject to control; to enslave.
To subordinate to someone or something.
Resembling a vassal; slavish; servile.
(grammar) A word that indicates an action, event, or state.
definitions.example: The word “speak” is an English verb.

definitions.definition: Any word; a vocable.
An action as opposed to a trait or thing.
definitions.example: Kindness is a verb, not an adjective. You're only kind if you do kind things.

definitions.definition: A named command that performs a specific operation on an object.
To use any word that is not, or had not been a verb (especially a noun) as if it were a verb.
(used as a neutral, unspecific verb) To perform any action that is normally expressed by a verb.
A plant of the genus Iris, common in the northern hemisphere, and generally having attractive blooms (See Iris (plant)).
The contractile membrane perforated by the pupil, which adjusts to control the amount of light reaching the retina, and which forms the colored portion of the eye (See Iris (anatomy)).
A diaphragm used to regulate the size of a hole, especially as a way of controlling the amount of light reaching a lens.
A rainbow, or other colourful refraction of light.
A constricted opening in the path inside a waveguide, used to form a resonator.
The inner circle of an oscillated color spot.
(of an aperture, lens or door) To open or close in the manner of an iris.
The striking of one body against another; collision.
The force or energy of a collision of two objects.
definitions.example: The hatchet cut the wood on impact.

definitions.definition: A forced impinging.
definitions.example: His spine had an impingement; L4 and L5 made impact, which caused numbness in his leg.

definitions.definition: A significant or strong influence; an effect.
definitions.example: His friend's opinion had an impact on his decision.

definitions.definition: To collide or strike, the act of impinging.
definitions.example: When the hammer impacts the nail, it bends.

definitions.definition: To compress; to compact; to press into something or pack together.
definitions.example: The footprints of birds do not impact the soil in the way those of dinosaurs do.

definitions.definition: To influence; to affect; to have an impact on.
definitions.example: I can make the changes, but it will impact the schedule.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress onto something.
definitions.example: Ideas impacted on the mind.

definitions.definition: To design a new process or mechanism.
definitions.example: After weeks of hard work, I invented a new way to alphabetize matchbooks.

definitions.definition: To create something fictional for a particular purpose.
definitions.example: I knew I had to invent an excuse, and quickly.

definitions.definition: To come upon; to find; to discover.
To set on fire; to kindle; to cause to burn, flame, or glow.
To kindle or intensify (a feeling, as passion or appetite); to excite to an excessive or unnatural action or heat.
definitions.example: to inflame desire

definitions.definition: To provoke (a person) to anger or rage; to exasperate; to irritate; to incense; to enrage.
To put in a state of inflammation; to produce morbid heat, congestion, or swelling, of.
definitions.example: to inflame the eyes by overwork

definitions.definition: To exaggerate; to enlarge upon.
To grow morbidly hot, congested, or painful; to become angry or incensed.
Resulting from or affected by inflammation.
Appearing to be in flames; as if made of fire.
The imagination.
An image or representation of anything formed in the mind.
An opinion or notion formed without much reflection.
A whim.
definitions.example: I had a fancy to learn to play the flute.

definitions.definition: Love or amorous attachment.
definitions.example: He took a fancy to her.

definitions.definition: The object of inclination or liking.
Any sport or hobby pursued by a group.
definitions.example: Trainspotting is the fancy of a special lot.

definitions.definition: The enthusiasts of such a pursuit.
definitions.example: He fell out of favor with the boxing fancy after the incident.

definitions.definition: A diamond with a distinctive colour.
That which pleases or entertains the taste or caprice without much use or value.
A sort of love song or light impromptu ballad.
In the game of jacks, a style of play involving additional actions (contrasted with plainsies).
Decorative.
definitions.example: This is a fancy shawl.

definitions.definition: Of a superior grade.
definitions.example: This box contains bottles of the fancy grade of jelly.

definitions.definition: Executed with skill.
definitions.example: He initiated the game winning play with a fancy, deked saucer pass to the winger.

definitions.definition: Unnecessarily complicated.
definitions.example: I'm not keen on him and his fancy ideas.

definitions.definition: Extravagant; above real value.
In a fancy manner; fancily.
The amount a sack will contain.
definitions.example: A sackful of sand won't help the soil here much, but a dump truck full would.

definitions.definition: A large number or amount (of something).
A mineral consisting of hydrated calcium sulphate. When calcined, it forms plaster of Paris.
To write a first version, make a preliminary sketch.
To draw in outline; to make a draught, sketch, or plan of, as in architectural and mechanical drawing.
To write a law.
To conscript a person, force a person to serve in some capacity, especially in the military.
definitions.example: He was drafted during the Vietnam War.

definitions.definition: To select someone (or something) for a particular role or purpose.
definitions.example: There was a campaign to draft Smith to run for President.

definitions.definition: To select and separate an animal or animals from a group.
definitions.example: The calves were drafted from the cows.

definitions.definition: To select a rookie player onto a professional sports team.
definitions.example: After his last year of college football, he was drafted by the Miami Dolphins.

definitions.definition: To follow very closely (behind another vehicle), thereby providing an aerodynamic advantage to both lead and follower and conserving energy or increasing speed.
To draw out; to call forth.
To draw fibers out of a clump, for spinning in the production of yarn.
The act or process of producing a technical drawing, or draft.
A profession, usually related to making technical drawings for architecture, engineering, or manufacturing.
The practice among ground vehicle drivers or cyclists of following in a close line to reduce wind resistance.
A method of preparing fibers for weaving.
To act or behave in an inappropriate, improper, incorrect, or unexpected manner.
definitions.example: He doesn't mean to misbehave; he just doesn't know better.

definitions.definition: To shovel muck.
definitions.example: We need to muck the stable before it gets too thick.

definitions.definition: To manure with muck.
To do a dirty job.
To pass, to fold without showing one's cards, often done when a better hand has already been revealed.
A doctor.
A paramedic, someone with special training in first aid, especially in the military.
A medical student.
Of or pertaining to medicines; medical.
To be ongoing (occurring, happening); to last, proceed or continue.
Something that is going on; a happening.
Continuing, permanent, lasting.
Presently or currently happening; being in progress.
To make something (such as an abstract idea) possible to be perceived by the senses.
To treat as something objectively real.
To treat as a mere object and deny the dignity of.
An act of going out or going away, or leaving; a departure.
definitions.example: He made his exit at the opportune time.

definitions.definition: A way out.
The act of departing from life; death.
definitions.example: the untimely exit of a respected politician

definitions.definition: To go out or go away from a place or situation; to depart, to leave.
To depart from life; to die.
To end or terminate (a program, subroutine, etc.)
(originally United States) To depart from or leave (a place or situation).
A mixture of bark, dried leaves and often berries and/or tobacco, which is smoked.
Any of the plants whose berries, leaves or bark used in this mixture.
definitions.example: He ate kinnikinnick berries.

definitions.definition: In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.
definitions.example: Penny was wearing a miniskirt, so she skinned her exposed knees when she fell.

definitions.definition: In the horse and allied animals, the carpal joint, corresponding to the wrist in humans.
The part of a garment that covers the knee.
A piece of timber or metal formed with an angle somewhat in the shape of the human knee when bent.
An act of kneeling, especially to show respect or courtesy.
definitions.example: To make a knee.

definitions.definition: Any knee-shaped item or sharp angle in a line; an inflection point.
definitions.example: the knee of a graph

definitions.definition: A blow made with the knee; a kneeing.
To kneel to.
definitions.example: 1605: I could as well be brought / To knee his throne and, squire-like, pension beg / To keep base life afoot. — William Shakespeare, King Lear II.ii

definitions.definition: To poke or strike with the knee.
To move on the knees; to use the knees to move.
Oneness; the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
Agreement; harmony.
A single undivided thing, seen as complete in itself.

Any of the three classical rules of drama: unity of action (nothing should be admitted not directly relevant to the development of the plot), unity of place (the scenes should be set in the same place), and unity of time (all the events should be such as might happen within a single day).
The number 1 or any element of a set or field that behaves under a given operation as the number 1 behaves under multiplication.
The peculiar characteristics of an estate held by several in joint tenancy.

The form of consensus in a Quaker meeting for business which signals that a decision has been reached. In order to achieve unity, everyone who does not agree with the decision must explicitly stand aside, possibly being recorded in the minutes as doing so.
Vomit.
To vomit.
definitions.example: Joel began to upchuck all over the grandstand.

definitions.definition: Something that breaks.
A machine for breaking rocks, or for breaking coal at the mines
The building in which such a machine is placed.
A person who specializes in breaking things.
(chiefly in the plural) A wave breaking into foam against the shore, or against a sandbank, or a rock or reef near the surface, considered a useful warning to ships of an underwater hazard
A breakdancer.
A user of CB radio.
One who works in shipbreaking.
To do bookkeeping.
The skill or practice of keeping books or systematic records of financial transactions, e.g. income and expenses.
General tasks for maintaining a system.
Heraldry
A place where arms are kept, an arsenal.
A collection of weapons and materiel.
A place where arms are made.
A person who performs, plays a part in a theatrical play or film.
One who acts; a doer.
One who takes part in a situation.
An advocate or proctor in civil courts or causes.
One who institutes a suit; plaintiff or complainant.
(policy debate) One who enacts a certain policy action.
The entity that performs a role (in use case analysis).
(grammar) The most agent-like argument of a clause, e.g. 'the torpedo' in "The torpedo sank the boat" and "The torpedo fired".
A deepwater fish of the genus Chauliodus, a fierce predator with long, needle-like teeth and hinged lower jaws, found mainly in tropical and temperate waters.
To express with the voice, to utter.
(of animals) To produce noises or calls from the throat.
definitions.example: We could hear the monkeys vocalizing, though we could not see them.

definitions.definition: To sing without using words.
To turn a consonant into a vowel.
definitions.example: In Hong Kong English, /l/ may be vocalized at the end of a syllable.

definitions.definition: To make a sound voiced rather than voiceless.
To add vowel points to a consonantal script (e.g. niqqud in Hebrew)
